name: pducontrol

metadata.name: pducontrol
metadata.summary: A simple package to control PDUs

documentation:
# pducontrol

pducontrol is a Python library to control a wide variety of PDUs
Currently we support the following models:

* SWH-1023J-08N1 (Dexlan, seems to be just an OEM rebadged unit)
* PDU81003 (CyberPower)
* NetIO 4C

## Installation

 You can install pducontrol from source:

```sh
make pip
pip install /dist/pducontrol-*.tar.gz
```
or just call the ``refresh_package.sh`` script

## Usage

This package provides both Python and CLI interfaces, here is how you can 
use them:

### In Python

```python
from pducontrol import PDUControlREST
pdu = PDUControlREST("192.168.0.23")
print(pdu.get_all_names())
```

### In CLI

```sh
pducontrol 192.168.0.23 get_name all
```

A configuration file named `pdu_settings.cfg`, containing IP addresses and
credentials can be added in`~/.config/pducontrol/` for ease of use and 
autocompletion. 

An example file is available in `config`.


## Contributing

Bug reports and comments are warmly welcome,
but contributions, such as pull requests and patches,
are not, for legal reasons only.
If that's bothering you, don't hesitate to tell, with enough pressure, we could
reconsider our position.

## License

pducontrol is released under LGPL v3

## Hacking

### Execute the program from the source directory

```sh
python3 -m pducontrol
```

### Generate a debian package

```sh
make debian
```

### Execute the unit tests

They are located in **tests/**.

```sh
make test
```

### Coding style

All python files should respect the PEP 8 coding style and shell scripts, the
shellcheck coding style.

Instantiate the correct class based on the PDU's IP

Args:

Returns:
    If found, an instance of a supported PDU, else returns None

classes.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.get_total_outlets
documentation:
This method exists to keep the same code structure as the one
used to control the ROC PDU

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.get_all_outlet_statuses
documentation:
Returns the state of all the outlets
Returns:
    list[str]: List containing the state of all outlets

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.get_outlet_status
documentation:
Returns the state of a specific outlet
Args:
    outlet: ID of the outlet (from 1 to 8)
    timeout: max time without answer

Returns
    str: State of the desired outlet

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.toggle_outlet
documentation:
Toggle a specific outlet
Args:
    outlet: ID of the outlet (from 1 to 8)
    state: The desired state of the outlets (on, off or reboot)

Returns
    str: The final state of the outlets

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.toggle_all_outlets
documentation:
Toggles all outlets
Args:
    state: The desired state of the outlets (on, off or reboot)

Returns
    str: The final state of the outlets

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.get_all_names
documentation:
Returns the name of all outlets

Returns
List str : The name of all outlets

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.get_name
documentation:
Returns the name of an outlet
Args:
    outlet: ID of the outlet (from 1 to 8)

Returns
str : The name of the desired outlet

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu_swh_1023j.PDUControlREST.set_name
documentation:
Sets the name of an outlet

Args:
    outlet: the outlet number
    name: the name to set (under 32 characters)
Class used to control the SWH 1023J PDU from the LU test farm

functions.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x._reverse
documentation:
return the dictionary passed in argument, in reverse order, that is,
inverting the roles of the keys and the values

classes.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for the PDU8100X class

Keyword Arguments:
ip -- IPv4 address of the PDU8100X device
version -- SNMP version to use ('1' or '2c')
user -- unused, here for cross-compatibility
password -- unused, here for cross-compatibility

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.get_total_outlets
documentation:
Gets the total number of controllable outputs

Returns
An integer representing the total number of controllable
outlets, or None on error

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.get_outlet_status
documentation:
Gets the active state of a controllable outlet

Keyword Arguments:
outlet -- The outlet number

Returns:
A string representing the active state of the outlet,
or None on error

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.get_all_outlet_statuses
documentation:
Gets the active state of all controllable outlets

Returns
A list of strings representing the active state of all the
outlets, or None on error

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.toggle_outlet
documentation:
Sets the active state of a controllable outlet

Keyword Arguments:
outlet -- The outlet number
state -- The active state to set the outlet to
         (on, off, reboot)

Returns:
A string representing the new active state of the
outlet, or None on error

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.toggle_all_outlets
documentation:
Sets the active state of a controllable outlet

Keyword Arguments:
state -- The active state to set the outlets to
         (on, off, reboot)

Returns:
A string representing the new active state of all
the outlets, or None on error

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.get_name
documentation:
Gets the name of an outlet

Keyword Arguments:
outlet - The outlet number

Returns:
A string containing the name of the outlet

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.set_name
documentation:
Sets the name of an outlet

Args:
    outlet: the outlet number
    name: the name to set (under 32 characters)

methods.name: pducontrol.__pdu8100x.PDU8100X.get_all_names
documentation: Gets the name of all outlets

Interface class for controlling the Cyberpower PDU8100X family of
devices via SNMP.

Notes
 - Datasheet can be found in the downloads section of the following
   page:
   https://www.cyberpowersystems.com/product/pdu/switched-mbo/pdu81006/
 - Only works on linux (as this package uses the 'snmpwalk' bash
   command)
 - Ensure the 'snmp' system package is installed

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.set_ip
documentation:
Sets the IP address to target SNMP requests to

Keyword Arguments:
ip -- The address of the target device

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.set_version
documentation:
Sets the SNMP version of the requests

Keyword Arguments:
version -- SNMP version of the request ('1', '2c', or '3')

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.__gen_snmpwalk_cmd
documentation:
Snmpwalk command to query the device

Keyword Arguments:
oid -- The SNMP object identifier to query

Returns:
A string representing the whole snmpwalk query

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.__gen_snmpset_cmd
documentation:
Snmpset command to write to the device

Keyword Arguments:
oid -- The SNMP object identifier to query
value_type -- The type of the value being written to the device
              (e.g. integer, string, etc..)
value -- The value being written to the device

Returns:
A string representing the whole snmpget query

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.snmpwalk
documentation:
Snmpwalk query to the device

Keyword Arguments:
oid -- The SNMP object identifier to query

Returns:
A string representing the output of the snmpwalk query or None
if an error occured

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.snmpset
documentation:
Snmpset query to the device

Keyword Arguments:
oid -- The SNMP object identifier to query
value_type -- The type of the value being written to the device
              (e.g. integer, string, etc..)
value -- The value being written to the device

Returns:
A string representing the output of the snmpset query or None
if an error occured

methods.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.parse_by_type
documentation:
Parser for snmpwalk/set return values based on value type

Keyword Arguments:
line -- The snmpwalk/set return value
value_type -- The type of the value being written to the device
              (e.g. integer, string, etc..)

Returns:
The parsed value. Currently either an integer or string

inner_classes.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.V3Args
documentation: Internal SNMP class to hold SNMPv3-specific configuration

inner_classes.name: pducontrol.__snmp.SNMPClient.V1V2Args
documentation: Internal SNMP class to hold SNMPv1/2c-specific configuration

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.get_credentials
documentation:
Returns a username and a password. Checks the parsed arguments and
 then config file.

Args:
    args: the previously parsed args
Returns:
    A username/password combo if found, else an empty dict

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_status
documentation:
Handles the status subcommand, outputs a table showing the state of all
power outlets

Args:
    args: the parsed command-line arguments
    pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.outlet_to_index
documentation:
function to convert outlet name into an index allowing designation by
outlet name in pdu control

Args:
    names: list of outlet name for a given IP address
    outlet: given command-line argument to convert

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.do_handle_set_and_power_cycle
documentation:
Helper function to ease the implementation of the set and power_cycle
subcommand handlers

Keyword Arguments:
args: the parsed command-line arguments
pdu: a supported pdu class object
action: callable performing the action on the targeted outlets

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_set
documentation:
Handles the set subcommand, sets the state of all the outlets
specified as arguments

Keyword Arguments:
args: the parsed command-line arguments
pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_power_cycle
documentation:
Handles the power_cycle subcommand, reboots all the outlets specified
as arguments

Keyword Arguments:
args: the parsed command-line arguments
pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_set_all
documentation:
Handles the set_all subcommand, changes state of all the outlets

Args:
    args: the parsed command-line arguments
    pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_power_cycle_all
documentation:
Handles the power_cycle_all subcommand, reboots all the outlets

Args:
    args: the parsed command-line arguments
    pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_get_name
documentation:
Handles the get_name subcommand, prints the name of the requested outlet

Args:
    args: the parsed command-line arguments
    pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.handle_set_name
documentation:
Handles the set_name subcommand, sets the name of the requested outlet

Args:
    args: the parsed command-line arguments
    pdu: a supported pdu class object

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.address_completer
documentation:
Argcomplete compatible completer function
Returns a list of IPs from the config file

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.name_to_address
documentation:
Transform the pdu name into ip address if name is present in the
config file

Args:
    ip_address : the ip_address or pdu's name command-line argument

Return ip_address string

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.complete_prefix
documentation:
Sub function used by name_completer function

Args:
    prefix: user prompt before pressing tab
    outlets: outlet's name list

Return a filtered list of outlet's name

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.name_completer
documentation:
Argcomplete compatible completer function

Args:
    prefix: user prompt before pressing tab
    parsed_args : the parsed command-line arguments

Return a list of outlet's name from given ip address

functions.name: pducontrol.__cli.get_parser
documentation:
Generate an argparse parser out of its description

Args:
    parser_desc: The description of the parser

Returns:
    The instantiated parser
